gmail_show_account_account_get.description:
description: Show your account information. Unlike show_profile, this includes private information.
model: User
---
success:
{
    "first_name": "string",
    "last_name": "string",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "registered_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
    "last_logged_in": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
    "status": "active"
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

gmail_signup_account_post.description:
description: Sign up to create account.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: Your first name

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: Your last name

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Your password.

gmail_delete_account_account_delete.description:
description: Delete your account.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

gmail_update_account_name_account_patch.description:
description: Update your first or last name in the account profile.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

gmail_logout_auth_logout_post.description:
description: Logout from your account.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

gmail_send_password_reset_code_send_pass.description:
description: Send password reset code to your email address.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

gmail_reset_password_reset_password_post.description:
description: Reset your password using the password reset code sent to your email address.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Your email address.

parameters.name: password_reset_code
parameters.description: The password reset code sent to your email address.

parameters.name: new_password
parameters.description: Your new password.

gmail_show_profile_profile_get.description:
description: Show public profile information of a user.
model: User
---
success:
{
    "first_name": "string",
    "last_name": "string",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "registered_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
    "status": "active"
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email of the person you want to see the profile information of.

gmail_set_status_status_post.description:
description: Set your availability status.
model: User
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Your availability status.
constraints: must be one of: [active, do_not_disturb, away]

gmail_search_users_users_get.description:
description: Search Gmail users by name or email address.
model: User
---
success:
[
    {
        "first_name": "string",
        "last_name": "string",
        "email": "user@example.com",
        "registered_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
        "status": "active"
    }
]
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query string.

parameters.name: page_index
parameters.description: The index of the page to return.

parameters.name: page_limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return per page.

gmail_search_labels_labels_get.description:
description: Search email thread labels by name.
model: UserEmailThread
---
success:
[
    "string"
]
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query string.

parameters.name: page_index
parameters.description: The index of the page to return.

parameters.name: page_limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return per page.

gmail_show_inbox_threads_email_threads_c.description:
description:
    Show or search email threads you have received.
    This will not show detailed information about emails within the thread.
model: UserEmailThread
---
success:
[
    {
        "email_thread_id": 1,
        "email_ids": [
            1
        ],
        "draft_ids": [
            1
        ],
        "incoming": true,
        "outgoing": true,
        "read": true,
        "label": "string",
        "snoozed_until": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
        "starred": true,
        "archived": true,
        "spam": true,
        "created_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
        "updated_at": "2019-01-01T00:00:00",
        "subject": "string",
        "participants": [
            {
                "name": "string",
                "email": "user@example.com"
            }
        ],
        "preview": "string",
        "has_attachments": true
    }
]
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string.

parameters.name: page_index
parameters.description: The index of the page to return.

parameters.name: page_limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: The label to filter the emails by.

parameters.name: starred
parameters.description: Filter emails by starred status.

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Filter emails by archived status.

parameters.name: spam
parameters.description: Filter emails by spam status.

parameters.name: snoozed
parameters.description: Filter emails by snoozed status.

parameters.name: read
parameters.description: Filter emails by read status.

parameters.name: attachment
parameters.description: Filter emails by whether they have an attachment or not.

parameters.name: from_email
parameters.description: Filter emails by sender email address.

parameters.name: to_email
parameters.description: Filter emails by recipient email address.

parameters.name: min_created_at
parameters.description: Filter emails by minimum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: max_created_at
parameters.description: Filter emails by maximum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: The attribute to sort the email threads by prefixed with +/- to reflect ascending/descending. Valid attributes: created_at and updated_at. If both query and sort_by are given and non-empty, results will be first ranked by query relevance, then paginated, and will then be sorted by the given attribute within each page. If both query and sort_by are not given, null, or empty, sort_by will default to -updated_at.

gmail_show_category_sizes_category_sizes.description:
description:
    Show the number of email threads in each category of inbox, outbox, archived, spam,
    and the number of unscheduled and scheduled email drafts.
model: UserEmailThread
---
success:
{
    "inbox": 1,
    "outbox": 1,
    "snoozed": 1,
    "starred": 1,
    "archived": 1,
    "spam": 1,
    "unscheduled_drafts": 1,
    "scheduled_drafts": 1
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: read
parameters.description: Count only read or unread email threads. If None, count both read and unread threads. It is not applicable for drafts.

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to show.

description: Delete an email thread.
model: UserEmailThread
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to delete.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email to show.

description: Mark an email thread as unarchived.
model: UserEmailThread

description: Mark an email thread as starred.

{"message": "string"}

gmail_mark_thread_unstarred_email_thread.description:
description: Mark an email thread as unstarred.
model: UserEmailThread
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to unstar.

gmail_delete_email_in_thread_email_threa.description:
description: Delete an email in a thread.
model: UserEmailThread
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to delete an email from.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email to delete.

gmail_snooze_thread_email_threads_email_.description:
description:
    Snooze an email thread until a given date and time in the future.
    It will reappear in unread state in your inbox and/or outbox at that time.
    It will also be removed from archived or spam categories if it was in either of those.
model: UserEmailThread
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to snooze.

parameters.name: snooze_until
parameters.description: The date and time to snooze the email thread until. The date and time should be in YYYY-MM-DD|HH:MM:SS format

gmail_unsnooze_thread_email_threads_emai.description:
description: Unsnooze an email thread.
model: UserEmailThread
---
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to unsnooze.

gmail_send_email_emails_post.description:
description: Send a new email to one or more recipients.
model: UserEmailThread
---
success:
{
    "message": "string",
    "sent_email_thread_id": 1,
    "sent_email_id": 1
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_addresses
parameters.description: List of recipient email addresses.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The subject of the email.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the email.

parameters.name: attachment_file_paths
parameters.description: List of absolute file paths (starting with /) from the file_system app to attach to the email.

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to reply to.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email to reply to.

parameters.name: attachment_file_paths
parameters.description: List of absolute file paths (starting with /) from the file_system app to attach to the email.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the reply email.

gmail_forward_email_from_thread_email_th.description:
description: Forward an email from an email thread to one or more recipients.
model: UserEmailThread
---
success:
{
    "message": "string",
    "sent_email_thread_id": 1,
    "sent_email_id": 1,
    "draft_id": 1
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to forward.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email to forward.

parameters.name: email_addresses
parameters.description: List of recipient email addresses.

parameters.name: draft_not_send
parameters.description: If true, the email will be saved as a draft instead of being sent. This way it can be edited before sending.

gmail_forward_email_thread_email_threads.description:
description: Forward entire email thread to one or more recipients.
model: UserEmailThread
---
success:
{
    "message": "string",
    "sent_email_thread_id": 1,
    "sent_email_id": 1,
    "draft_id": 1
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: email_thread_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email thread to forward.

parameters.name: email_addresses
parameters.description: List of recipient email addresses.

parameters.name: draft_not_send
parameters.description: If true, the email will be saved as a draft instead of being sent. This way it can be edited before sending.

parameters.name: recipient_email_addresses
parameters.description: List of recipient email addresses.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the draft.

parameters.name: attachment_file_paths
parameters.description: List of absolute file paths (starting with /) from the file_system app to attach to the draft.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string.

parameters.name: page_index
parameters.description: The index of the page to return.

parameters.name: page_limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: recipient_email
parameters.description: Filter emails by recipient email address.

parameters.name: attachment
parameters.description: Filter drafts by attachment status.

parameters.name: scheduled
parameters.description: Filter drafts by whether they are scheduled to be sent in the future.

parameters.name: belongs_to_email_thread_id
parameters.description: Filter drafts by email thread ID that the draft belongs to.

parameters.name: response_to_email_id
parameters.description: Filter drafts by email ID that the draft responds to.

parameters.name: min_created_at
parameters.description: Filter drafts by minimum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: max_created_at
parameters.description: Filter drafts by maximum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: The attribute to sort the drafts by prefixed with +/- to reflect ascending/descending. Valid attributes: created_at and updated_at. If both query and sort_by are given and non-empty, results will be first ranked by query relevance, then paginated, and will then be sorted by the given attribute within each page. If both query and sort_by are not given, null, or empty, sort_by will default to -updated_at.

parameters.name: draft_id

gmail_send_email_from_draft_drafts_draft.description:
description: Send a new email from a draft right away.
model: Draft
---
success:
{
    "message": "string",
    "sent_email_thread_id": 1,
    "sent_email_id": 1
}
failure:
{"message": "string"}

parameters.name: draft_id
parameters.description: The ID of the draft to send.

parameters.name: attachment_id
parameters.description: The ID of the attachment to download.

parameters.name: download_to_file_path
parameters.description: The file path to download the attachment to in file system app. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'. If not passed, it will be saved in your ~/downloads directory.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists.

parameters.name: draft_id
parameters.description: The ID of the draft to upload the attachment to.

parameters.name: attachment_file_paths
parameters.description: List of absolute file paths (starting with /) from the file_system app to attach to the email.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Whether to overwrite the attachment if it already exists.
